questions.text: Which of the following are incorrect regarding uterine manipulator benefits during laparoscopic hysterectomy? Select all that are incorrect.

answerOptions.text: A) Manipulators do not act as a second assistant by repositioning the uterus to expose surgical landmarks
B) Elevation and retroversion of the uterus improves access to the bladder flap.
C) Inward pressure increases distance between uterine arteries and ureters.
D) The colpotomy cup provides a tactile landmark for vaginal entry.
E) Manipulators decrease visibility of key pelvic structures.

questions.explanation: Manipulators enhance, not reduce, visualization; they also aid exposure and safety. Select statements that are incorrect.

questions.text: Which of the following statements are incorrect regarding the use of carbon dioxide (CO₂) for laparoscopy? Select all that are incorrect.

answerOptions.text: A) CO₂ is flammable and must be used cautiously with electrosurgery.
B) CO₂ is excreted through respiration.
C) CO₂ has a low diffusion capacity.
D) CO₂ is the most commonly used insufflation gas.
E) CO₂ absorption can lead to hypercapnia.

questions.explanation: CO₂ is nonflammable with high diffusion capacity; it’s absorbed and excreted via the lungs. Select the incorrect statements.
questions.submodule: Module 3.7 – Use of Carbon Dioxide During Laparoscopy

questions.text: Which of the following strategies help prevent fluid overload during operative hysteroscopy? Select all that apply.

answerOptions.text: A) Use of glycine instead of saline.
B) Monitoring fluid deficit carefully throughout the case.
D) Maintaining intrauterine pressure below mean arterial pressure.

questions.explanation: Track deficit, use vasopressin judiciously, keep pressure below MAP, and work efficiently. Avoid hypotonic glycine when saline is appropriate.

questions.text: Which of the following are incorrect about monopolar electrosurgery during hysteroscopy? Select all that are incorrect.

answerOptions.text: A) Monopolar loops are compatible with saline irrigation.
B) Glycine is an appropriate distension medium for monopolar electrosurgery.
C) A bipolar loop in a monopolar sheath will function normally.
D) Malfunction may occur if grounding pads are improperly applied.
E) Monopolar and bipolar components are generally incompatible.

questions.explanation: Saline disperses monopolar current (so A is incorrect); mixing bipolar electrodes in monopolar systems fails (C incorrect). Glycine is appropriate for monopolar; poor pad placement can malfunction; mono/bipolar parts are generally incompatible.
questions.submodule: Module 3.9 – Principles of Monopolar Electrosurgery in Operative Hysteroscopy

questions.text: Which 3 techniques help minimize fluid overload during operative hysteroscopy? Select 3 best answers.

answerOptions.text: B) Keeping intrauterine pressure below MAP
C) Use of glycine as distension media

questions.explanation: Use isotonic saline, keep pressure below MAP, and track deficit closely. Glycine increases risk; vasopressin and scope size changes aren’t primary strategies here.

questions.text: Which of the following is a common benefit of using a uterine manipulator during laparoscopic hysterectomy?

answerOptions.text: C) Improves pelvic exposure and anatomical landmarks
D) Reduces need for electrosurgical instruments

questions.explanation: Manipulators improve exposure and define landmarks for safe dissection.

questions.text: Which manipulator motion is most useful for identifying and developing the vesicouterine space?
questions.explanation: Retroversion exposes the vesicouterine fold to create the bladder flap.

questions.text: What feature of the colpotomy cup is most critical for a safe colpotomy during TLH?

answerOptions.text: D) Visual and physical delineation of the vaginal–cervical junction
E) Ability to seal off fluid leakage

questions.explanation: The cup defines the colpotomy plane, reducing injury risk.

questions.text: Which is a key advantage of carbon dioxide as an insufflation gas?

answerOptions.text: B) Excretion through the renal system
E) High toxicity to peritoneal tissue

questions.explanation: CO₂ is absorbed and eliminated via the lungs, and is nonflammable.
questions.submodule: Module 3.7 – Use of CO₂ in Laparoscopy

questions.text: Which patient is most at risk for complications from CO₂ insufflation during laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Elderly patients with cardiac disease are more sensitive to CO₂ absorption/hemodynamic effects.
questions.submodule: Module 3.7 – Use of CO₂ in Laparoscopy

questions.text: Primary physiologic effect of excess CO₂ absorption during laparoscopy:
questions.explanation: CO₂ absorption elevates PaCO₂ causing respiratory acidosis.
questions.submodule: Module 3.7 – Use of CO₂ in Laparoscopy

questions.text: Which distension media is least likely to contribute to hyponatremia?
questions.explanation: Isotonic saline with electrolytes minimizes hyponatremia risk.

questions.text: Which intraoperative measure is most effective at preventing excessive fluid absorption?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of room-temperature distension media
B) Keeping the hysteroscope in motion
C) Maintaining intrauterine pressure below mean arterial pressure
D) Performing cervical dilation prior to procedure

questions.explanation: Staying below MAP reduces intravasation through venous sinuses.

questions.text: What is the maximum safe concentration of vasopressin recommended to reduce fluid absorption risk during hysteroscopy?
questions.explanation: Keep vasopressin at or below ~0.4 units/mL to minimize cardiovascular risk.

questions.text: Monopolar electrosurgery requires which of the following to complete the circuit?
questions.explanation: A return electrode completes and safely disperses monopolar current.

questions.text: Which would most likely explain complete failure of monopolar current delivery during hysteroscopy despite pressing the foot pedal?

answerOptions.text: B) Bipolar electrode in a monopolar sheath
C) Saline being used as distension media
D) Sheath too narrow for electrode tip

questions.explanation: A mono–bipolar mismatch breaks the circuit. Saline disperses current but doesn’t usually prevent delivery entirely.

questions.text: Most likely clinical consequence of repeatedly activating monopolar electrosurgery in saline-filled cavities:

answerOptions.text: D) Diffuse thermal spread and tissue injury
E) Improved visualization due to fluid clarity

questions.explanation: In conductive saline, current disperses and can cause unintended thermal injury.
